content_of_post_to_comment: I just told my mum that I don’t want my dad in the background of our calls and I won’t join them if he is in earshot. I feel free and like I advocated for myself. He has so far gotten away with how he has treated me and gets to say we are still in contact by sliding into calls between my mum and him. I am done.
comment: One thing I've noticed is that boundaries become real when they change someone else's convenience, not just our own behavior. That's often the moment they're treated as conflict instead of clarity.
